column8: description

THESE FUNDS SUPPORT THE FURTHER DEVELOPMENT AND ADMINISTRATION OF THE TRIBE'S WATER QUALITY STANDARDS AND CERTIFICATIONS PROGRAM. THE OBJECTIVE OF THIS PROGRAM IS TO FINALIZE TRIBE'S WATER QUALITY STANDARDS FOR EPA APPROVAL. THE TRIBE WILL ALSO PERFORM OUTREACH AND EDUCATION TO THE TRIBAL COMMUNITY ON THE PURPOSE AND IMPORTANCE OF DEVELOPING WATER QUALITY STANDARDS.   THIS ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT PROVIDES FULL FEDERAL FUNDING IN THE AMOUNT OF $28,500.

column5: DEPARTMENT OF CONSERVATION AND NATURAL RESOURCES
column8: THIS AGREEMENT PROVIDES MULTIPURPOSE GRANT FUNDING FOR THE FOLLOWING FOUR (4) HIGH PRIORITY ACTIVITIES, TO COMPLEMENT EXISTING ENVIRONMENTAL GRANT PROGRAMS: NEVADA PRIORITY WETLAND INVENTORY UPDATES, HOUSEHOLD MERCURY RECOVERY AND DISPOSAL EVENTS, EXPLORATION OF MICROPLASTICS IN LAKE TAHOE, AND ACQUISITION OF NONPOINT SOURCE EDUCATIONAL SUPPLIES AND OUTREACH SPEAKERS.  THIS AGREEMENT PROVIDES FULL FEDERAL FUNDING IN THE AMOUNT OF $111,612.  SEE TERMS AND CONDITIONS.  PREAWARD COSTS HAVE BEEN APPROVED BACK TO OCTOBER 1, 2019.

THE DRINKING WATER TRIBAL SET-ASIDE (DWTSA) FUNDS ARE BEING AWARDED TO THE NAVAJO NATION TO FUND THE OPERATOR CERTIFICATION PROGRAM.  THIS PROGRAM ENSURES WATER OPERATORS ARE CERTIFIED TO THE LEVEL REQUIRED OF THE PUBLIC WATER SYSTEM THEY OPERATE AND MAINTAIN.  TRAINING, EXAMINATIONS, AND TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE ARE PROVIDED BY THE NAVAJO OPERATOR CERTIFICATION PROGRAM.  THIS ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT PROVIDES FULL FEDERAL FUNDING IN THE AMOUNT OF $125,000.  PRE-AWARD COSTS APPROVED BACK TO NOVEMBER 1, 2019.

THIS AGREEMENT PROVIDES FUNDING TO IMPLEMENT A PROGRAM OF VOLUNTARY TESTING FOR LEAD IN DRINKING WATER AT LOCAL CHILD CARE CENTERS. SPECIFICALLY, THE RECIPIENT WILL CONDUCT DRINKING WATER TESTING, PROVIDE EDUCATIONAL OUTREACH TO THE CHILD CARE CENTER COMMUNITY ON LEAD HAZARDS, COMMUNICATE TESTING RESULTS, AND SUPPORT FOLLOW-UP CORRECTIONS AS NEEDED.  THIS ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT PROVIDES FULL FEDERAL FUNDING IN THE AMOUNT OF $6,137,000.  SEE TERMS AND CONDITIONS.  PREAWARD COSTS HAVE BEEN APPROVED BACK TO JULY 1, 2020.

THIS AGREEMENT SUPPORTS THE FURTHER DEVELOPMENT AND ADMINISTRATION OF THE SHOSHONE PAIUTE TRIBES OF DUCK VALLEY'S NONPOINT SOURCE POLLUTION CONTROL PROGRAM.  MAJOR ACTIVITIES INCLUDE ASSESSMENT OF CURRENT NONPOINT SOURCE POLLUTION ISSUES THROUGHOUT THE RESERVATION AND UPDATING THE TRIBE'S NONPOINT SOURCE ASSESSMENT REPORT AND MANAGEMENT PROGRAM PLAN, WHICH WILL ULTIMATELY REDUCE NONPOINT SOURCE POLLUTANT LOADS TO THE TRIBAL WATERS.  THIS AGREEMENT PROVIDES FULL FEDERAL FUNDING IN THE AMOUNT OF $30,000.

THIS COOPERATIVE AGREEMENT WILL SUPPORT THE BORDER ENVIRONMENT INFRASTRUCTURE FUND (BEIF) PROGRAM.  THIS PROGRAM PROVIDES FUNDS FOR DESIGN AND CONSTRUCTION OF COMMUNITY WATER AND WASTEWATER INFRASTRUCTURE PROJECTS WITHIN 100 KILOMETERS NORTH AND SOUTH OF THE US-MEXICO BORDER AND FOR ADMINISTRATIVE AND RELATED COSTS TO THE IMPLEMENTATION AND OVERSIGHT OF THESE PROJECTS.  THIS ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT PROVIDES PARTIAL FEDERAL FUNDING IN THE AMOUNT OF $6,024,473.

column5: CONFEDERATED TRIBES OF THE GOSHUTE RESERVATION
column8: THIS COOPERATIVE AGREEMENT PROVIDES FUNDING TO DEVELOP THE TRIBE'S ENVIRONMENTAL PROGRAM. ACTIVITIES TO INCLUDE COMMUNITY OUTREACH ON COMPOSTING, UPDATE THE INTEGRATED SOLID WASTE MANAGEMENT PLAN, FOLLOW UP ON THE BROWNFIELD ASSESSMENT, CONDUCT AN ASSESSMENT OF THREE BURIED/PARTIALLY BURIED OPEN DUMPS, UPDATE THE INTEGRATED SOLID WASTE MANAGEMENT PLAN AND ATTEND MEETINGS AND TRAININGS IN ORDER TO DEVELOP CAPACITY IN THEIR ENVIRONMENTAL PROGRAMS.  THIS AGREEMENT PROVIDES FULL FEDERAL FUNDING IN THE AMOUNT OF $112,383.

THE PROPOSED PROJECT IS TO REPLACE WATERLINE INFRASTRUCTURE WITHIN THE ENTERPRISE MOBILE HOME PARK (MHP) ON THE CHEMEHUEVI INDIAN RESERVATION. WATER METERS WITH AUTOMATED METER READING CAPABILITY WILL ALSO BE INSTALLED AT EACH SERVICE LINE. THE NEW PIPE WILL PROVIDE SUSTAINABLE WATER SUPPLY TO THE COMMUNITY.  THIS ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT PROVIDES FULL FEDERAL FUNDING IN THE AMOUNT OF $435,700.

THESE FUNDS WILL HELP THE GRANTEE ACT AS A TECHNICAL EXPERT FOR THE BENEFIT OF THE NAVAJO NATION ON MINE SITES ON ABANDONED URANIUM MINE SITES THAT POSE A PUBLIC HEALTH EMERGENCY FOR THE NAVAJO PEOPLE. THE GRANTEE WILL HELP PROVIDE INFORMATION TO REQUESTERS SUCH AS EPA, PRINCIPLE RESPONSIBLE PARTIES, FEDERAL AGENCIES, AND ASSIST WITH SITE INVESTIGATION AND RESPONSE ACTIVITY. THE GRANTEE IS EXPECTED TO PARTICIPATE IN THE TEN-YEAR-PLAN COORDINATION AND REPORTING.  THIS ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT PROVIDES PARTIAL FEDERAL FUNDING IN THE AMOUNT OF $415,000.

THIS PROJECT WILL PROVIDE ASSISTANCE TO IDENTIFY AND ESTABLISH TWO NEAR-ROAD NITROGEN DIOXIDE (NO2) MONITORING SITES.  THIS SPECIFIC AWARD SUPPORTS ESTABLISHING A SITE IN PIMA COUNTY.  THIS ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT PROVIDES FEDERAL FUNDING IN THE AMOUNT OF $200,000.  PRE-AWARD COSTS HAVE BEEN APPROVED BACK TO FEBRUARY 1, 2020.

THIS AGREEMENT WILL PROVIDE ASSISTANCE TO THE AMERICAN SAMOA POWER AUTHORITY (ASPA) IN ITS EFFORTS TO REDUCE DIESEL EMISSIONS AND EXPOSURE THROUGHOUT THE TERRITORY OF AMERICAN SAMOA. ASPA WILL PURCHASE AND USE ELECTRIC VEHICLES AND A CHARGING STATION, ELIMINATING THE USE OF FOUR LIGHT DUTY DIESEL TRUCKS, WITH THE GOAL OF REDUCING THE PUBLIC'S EXPOSURE TO HARMFUL DIESEL EXHAUST.  THIS ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT PROVIDES FULL FEDERAL FUNDING IN THE AMOUNT OF $118,038.  SEE TERMS AND CONDITIONS.

THESE FUNDS WILL HELP GUAM ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION AGENCY (GEPA) REPLACE THREE OLD DIESEL-FUELED SCHOOL BUSES WITH THE THREE NEW CLEANER DIESEL-FUELED SCHOOL BUSES MEETING EPA'S ELIGIBLE DIESEL EMISSION STANDARDS. THE THREE OLD DIESEL SCHOOL BUSES OF A SIMILAR ENGINE SIZE, WHICH ARE CURRENTLY OPERATING, WILL BE PROPERLY SCRAPPED AND RENDERED INOPERABLE UNDER PROGRAM REQUIREMENTS.  THIS ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT PROVIDES FULL FEDERAL FUNDING IN THE AMOUNT OF $78,849.  SEE TERMS AND CONDITIONS.

THE PROJECT WILL UPGRADE THE EXISTING WASTEWATER TREATMENT PLANT (WWTP) TO REDUCE THE AMOUNT OF NUTRIENTS AND OTHER POLLUTANTS THAT ARE DISCHARGED IN NAVAJO NATION. THE PROJECT INCLUDES UPGRADES TO THE HEADWORKS FACILITY, A NEW CONTROL SYSTEM, UPGRADES TO THE AERATION SYSTEM, SLUDGE PAD, BELT FILTER PRESS BUILDING, AND A DISINFECTION CHAMBER.  THE RESULTING EFFLUENT WILL BE OF HIGHER QUALITY.  ALL WORK WILL  BE WITHIN THE FOOTPRINT OF THE EXISTING KAYENTA WWTP.   THIS ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT PROVIDES FULL FEDERAL FUNDING IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,261,000.

THE PROJECT WILL UPGRADE THE EXISTING WASTEWATER TREATMENT PLANT TO REDUCE THE AMOUNT OF NUTRIENTS AND OTHER POLLUTANTS THAT ARE DISCHARGED IN NAVAJO NATION. THE PROJECT INCLUDES UPGRADES TO THE HEADWORKS FACILITY, A NEW CONTROL SYSTEM, SLUDGE PAD, AND BELT FILTER PRESS BUILDING.  THE RESULTING EFFLUENT WILL BE OF HIGHER QUALITY.  ALL WORK WILL BE WITHIN THE FOOTPRINT OF THE EXISTING CHINLE WWTP.  THIS ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT PROVIDES FULL FEDERAL FUNDING IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,660,000.

THIS PROJECT IS FOR THE CONSTRUCTION OF THE FINAL CONNECTION FROM THE NAVAJO TRIBAL UTILITY AUTHORITY (NTUA) METER BOX AT THE BOUNDARY OF THE DZIL LIBEI ELEMENTARY SCHOOL TO THE WATER SYSTEM SERVING THE ELEMENTARY SCHOOL.  THIS DIRECT CONNECTION WILL ELIMINATE THE NEED FOR THE TUBA CITY UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT TO MAINTAIN A SEPARATE WELL, STORAGE TANK, AND BOOSTER PUMPING SYSTEM FOR DRINKING WATER PURPOSES AT THE ELEMENTARY SCHOOL.  THE FUNDING WILL ALSO BE USED TO MAKE MODIFICATIONS TO THE EXISTING WATER SYSTEM AT THE ELEMENTARY SCHOOL SO THERE ARE NO CROSS CONNECTIONS BETWEEN THE POTABLE WATER AND FIRE SUPPRESSION SYSTEMS.  THIS ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT PROVIDES FULL FEDERAL FUNDING IN THE AMOUNT OF $168,425.

THE PROJECT IS LOCATED WITHIN THE SALT RIVER PIMA MARICOPA INDIAN COMMUNITY (COMMUNITY) IN THE DOBSON HEIGHTS SUBDIVISION. THE DOBSON HEIGHTS SUBDIVISION WAS CONSTRUCTED IN THE LATE 1990S AND INCLUDES 28 HOMES AND A NEIGHBORHOOD CENTER. THERE IS AN EXISTING 4'' ASBESTOS CEMENT (AC) WATERLINE THAT RUNS ALONG THE NORTH SIDE OF PINCHOT DR.  THE SUBDIVISION IS LOCATED AT THE INTERSECTION OF DOBSON AND PINCHOT ROAD. THE RESIDENTS WITHIN THIS SUBDIVISION HAVE EXPERIENCED NUMEROUS WATERLINE BREAKS DUE TO THE EXISTING CONDITION OF THE WATER MAIN. THE NEW 8'' PVC (OR DIP) WATERLINE (WITH SERVICES) WILL BE CONSTRUCTED ON PINCHOT RD.   THIS ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT PROVIDES FULL FEDERAL FUNDING IN THE AMOUNT OF $536,760.

THIS PROJECT PROVIDES FUNDING FOR THE TRIBE'S RESPONSE PROGRAM THAT INCLUDES TIMELY SURVEY AND INVENTORY OF BROWNFIELD SITES, WHICH ARE PROPERTIES WHOSE EXPANSION, REDEVELOPMENT OR REUSE MAY BE COMPLICATED BY THE PRESENCE OF HAZARDOUS SUBSTANCES. IT ALSO INCLUDES OVERSIGHT AND ENFORCEMENT AUTHORITIES TO ENSURE THAT RESPONSE ACTIONS PROTECT HUMAN HEALTH AND THE ENVIRONMENT; RESOURCES TO PROVIDE MEANINGFUL PUBLIC INVOLVEMENT; MECHANISMS FOR APPROVAL OF CLEANUP PLANS, AND VERIFICATION OF COMPLETE RESPONSES.    THIS AGREEMENT PROVIDES FULL FEDERAL FUNDING IN THE AMOUNT OF $75,000.  SEE TERMS AND CONDITIONS.

DESCRIPTION:THE AGREEMENT PROVIDES FUNDING FOR THE OPERATION OF THE WHITE MOUNTAIN APACHE TRIBE'S CONTINUING ENVIRONMENTAL PROGRAMS WHILE GIVING IT GREATER FLEXIBILITY TO ADDRESS ITS HIGHEST ENVIRONMENTAL PRIORITIES, IMPROVE ENVIRONMENTAL PERFORMANCE, ACHIEVE ADMINISTRATIVE SAVINGS AND STRENGTHEN THE PARTNERSHIP BETWEEN THE WHITE MOUNTAIN APACHE TRIBE AND EPA. THIS AGREEMENT FUNDS TRIBAL PROGRAMS FOR CLEAN WATER ACT (CWA) 106 AND CWA 319 BASE AND COMPETITIVE PROGRAMS.  SPECIFICALLY, THIS GRANT WILL PROVIDE FUNDS TO IMPLEMENT A WATER QUALITY MONITORING PROGRAM, ANALYZE WATER QUALITY DATA, NONPOINT SOURCE RESTORATION AND TRAIN TRIBAL STAFF, AMONG OTHER TASKS. 

THIS ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT PROVIDES FULL FEDERAL FUNDING IN THE AMOUNT OF $360,000. THE CWA 106 AND 319 BASE WORKPLAN ACTIVITIES ARE FULLY FUNDED THROUGH 9/30/2025. SEE TERMS AND CONDITIONS. 

NOTE: $125,000 IN TRIBAL CLEAN WATER ACT SECTION 319 COMPETITIVE FUNDS UNDER EPA-OW-OWOW-24-01 IS OBLIGATED AS PART OF THIS FUNDING ACTION. ACTIVITIES:THIS AGREEMENT FUNDS TRIBAL PROGRAMS FOR CLEAN WATER ACT (CWA) 106 AND CWA 319 BASE AND COMPETITIVE PROJECT. SPECIFICALLY, THIS GRANT WILL FUND THE IMPLEMENTATION OF A WATER QUALITY MONITORING PROGRAM, ANALYSIS OF WATER QUALITY DATA, NONPOINT SOURCE RESTORATION, COMPETITIVE NONPOINT SOURCE FENCING PROJECT AND TRAINING OF TRIBAL STAFF, AMONG OTHER TASKS.SUBRECIPIENT:NO SUBAWARDS ARE INCLUDED IN THIS ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT.OUTCOMES:THE ANTICIPATED DELIVERABLES INCLUDE WATER QUALITY DATA AS A RESULT FROM WATER SAMPLING, VIRTUAL FENCING NONPOINT SOURCE RESTORATION PROJECT AND TRIBAL EMPLOYEE PROFESSIONAL DEVELOPMENT. DIRECT BENEFICIARIES OF THIS PROGRAM ARE THE CITIZENS OF THE WHITE MOUNTAIN APACHE TRIBE.

THE GRANTEE'S MAJOR ACTIVITIES FOR THIS AGREEMENT INCLUDES: IMPLEMENTING WATER QUALITY MONITORING AND ANALYSIS; IMPLEMENTING A NONPOINT SOURCE PROGRAM TO REDUCE IMPACTS TO SURFACE WATER; CONDUCTING OUTREACH AND EDUCATION TO THE COMMUNITY; AND GENERAL ADMINISTRATION OF THE TRIBE'S WATER QUALITY MANAGEMENT PROGRAM. THESE FUNDS SUPPORT THE FURTHER DEVELOPMENT AND ADMINISTRATION OF THE WHITE MT. APACHE TRIBE'S WATER POLLUTION CONTROL AND NONPOINT SOURCE MANAGEMENT PROGRAMS.  THIS AGREEMENT PROVIDES FULL FEDERAL FUNDING IN THE AMOUNT OF $200,000.  SEE TERMS AND CONDITIONS.

THIS AGREEMENT WILL PROVIDE FUNDING TO THE CITY OF LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA TO CLEAN UP A BROWNFIELD SITE AT RIVER PARK, LOCATED AT 2850 KERR STREET, LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA. ENCOMPASSING 12.5 ACRES WITHIN A 42-ACRE FORMER RAILYARD PROPERTY, THE SITE LIES ALONG THE LOS ANGELES RIVER AND IS WITHIN A QUALIFIED OPPORTUNITY ZONE. THE SITE IS CONTAMINATED WITH METALS, ARSENIC, AND VOLATILE AND SEMI-VOLATILE ORGANIC COMPOUNDS CO-MINGLED WITH PETROLEUM HYDROCARBONS.  THIS ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT PROVIDES FULL FEDERAL FUNDING IN THE AMOUNT OF $500,000.00.

column5: SAN JOAQUIN VALLEY UNIFIED AIR POLLUTION CONTROL DISTRICT
column8: THE PURPOSE OF THIS COOPERATIVE AGREEMENT IS TO REPLACE DIESEL AGRICULTURE TRACTORS WITH TIER 4 ENGINES IN THE SAN JOAQUIN VALLEY.  THIS ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT PROVIDES FULL FEDERAL FUNDING IN THE AMOUNT OF $5,000,000.00.

column5: SAN JOAQUIN VALLEY UNIFIED AIR POLLUTION CONTROL DISTRICT
column8: THE PURPOSE OF THIS COOPERATIVE AGREEMENT IS TO REPLACE HEAVY-DUTY DIESEL TRUCKS WITH HEAVY-DUTY ELECTRIC TRUCKS IN THE SAN JOAQUIN VALLEY.  THIS ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT PROVIDES FULL FEDERAL FUNDING IN THE AMOUNT OF $5,000,000.00.

column5: DEPARTMENT OF CONSERVATION AND NATURAL RESOURCES
column8: THIS AGREEMENT WILL PROVIDE ASSISTANCE TO THE NEVADA DIVISION OF ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (NDEP) IN ITS EFFORTS TO REDUCE DIESEL EMISSIONS AND EXPOSURE THROUGHOUT THE STATE OF NEVADA. NDEP WILL REPLACE TEN PROGRAM-ELIGIBLE DIESEL-POWERED SCHOOL BUSES AND FIVE PROGRAM-ELIGIBLE DIESEL-POWERED MUNICIPAL SERVICE VEHICLES WITH CLEANER VEHICLES. THIS PROJECT WILL REDUCE EMISSIONS OF DIESEL PARTICULATE MATTER AND OTHER POLLUTANTS SUCH AS NITROGEN OXIDES.  THIS ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT PROVIDES FEDERAL FUNDING IN THE AMOUNT OF $477,788.

column5: CONSERVATION AND NATURAL RESOURCES, NEVADA DEPARTMENT OF
column8: THIS PROJECT WILL EXPAND KNOWLEDGE OF THE LOCATIONS, EXTENT, AND STATUS OF ISOLATED AND HEADWATER SPRINGS, SPRINGS-DEPENDENT SPECIES, AND PRIORITY WETLANDS. THE PROJECT WILL RESULT IN AN INCREASE IN THE NUMBER OF KNOWN SPRING LOCATIONS AND THEIR CONDITION, IMPROVED KNOWLEDGE OF KNOWN SPRING LOCATIONS, IMPROVED WETLAND INVENTORIES AND BASELINE CONDITION ASSESSMENTS AND DATA, INCREASED KNOWLEDGE OF THE STATUS OF SPRINGS- AND WETLAND-DEPENDENT SENSITIVE SPECIES, AND INCREASED AWARENESS OF WETLANDS PROGRAM.  THIS AGREEMENT PROVIDES PARTIAL FEDERAL FUNDING IN THE AMOUNT OF $193,672.  SEE TERMS AND CONDITIONS.

THIS PROJECT WILL CREATE CONSERVATION FRAMEWORKS FOR 2-4 LARGE HABITAT BLOCKS IN CALIFORNIA'S CENTRAL VALLEY AND MODOC PLATEAU REGIONS.  THE APPLICANT WILL CONVENE A TECHNICAL ADVISORY WORKGROUP, IDENTIFY EXTANT VERNAL POOL HABITAT BLOCKS BY ECOREGION, DEVELOP ECOREGION PROFILES AND CONSERVATION STRATEGY FRAMEWORKS FOR EACH HABITAT BLOCK AND THEN PREPARE A FINAL REPORT TO BE SHARED AND DISSEMINATED.  THIS ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT PROVIDES PARTIAL FEDERAL FUNDING IN THE AMOUNT OF $120,291.  SEE TERMS AND CONDITIONS.

column5: SOUTHERN CALIFORNIA COASTAL WATER RESEARCH PROJECT AUTHORITY
column8: THIS PROJECT WILL DEVELOP A SUITE OF MOLECULAR (DNA-BASED) LEVEL 3 INDICATORS AND METHODS THAT DIRECTLY MEASURE STREAM ECOSYSTEM FUNCTIONING IN A RAPID, COMPREHENSIVE, AND COST-EFFECTIVE MANNER. THIS PROJECT WILL ALSO PROVIDE A TOOL THAT CAN BE USED TO EVALUATE POTENTIAL CAUSAL STRESSORS DUE TO NUTRIENT IMPACTS, AND TO ASSESS THE PERFORMANCE OF COMPENSATORY MITIGATION PROJECTS.  THIS AGREEMENT PROVIDES PARTIAL FEDERAL FUNDS IN THE AMOUNT OF $149,863.

THE PROJECT INCLUDES RIPARIAN ASSESSMENT AND PRIORITIZATION TOOLS NEEDED TO SUPPORT THE PROTECTION OF RIVERS AND ASSOCIATED WETLANDS. THE RECIPIENT WILL VALIDATE THE RIPARIAN RAPID ASSESSMENT METHOD (RIPRAM) TOOL, DEVELOP AND TEST WATERSHED CONDITION ESTIMATION MODELS, DEVELOP RIPARIAN ZONE PROTECTION AND MANAGEMENT GOALS, IMPROVE DECISION MAKER ACCESS TO WATERSHED CONDITION DATA/INFORMATION, AND TRACK PROGRESS TOWARDS MEETING CENTRAL AND SOUTHERN CALIFORNIA RIPARIAN PROTECTION AND ENHANCEMENT GOALS.  THIS AGREEMENT PROVIDES PARTIAL FEDERAL FUNDING IN THE AMOUNT OF $249,286.

THE CHICKEN RANCH RANCHERIA OF ME-WUK INDIANS OF CALIFORNIA WANTS TO DEVELOP A WETLAND PROTECTION PROGRAM. IN ORDER TO HELP ACHIEVE THIS GOAL, THE TRIBE WILL CREATE A WETLANDS PROGRAM PLAN, BUILD STAFF CAPACITY TO DELINEATE WETLANDS, BUILD STAFF CAPACITY TO MONITOR WETLANDS, CREATE A WETLAND DELINEATION QUALITY ASSURANCE PROJECT PLAN (QAPP), CREATE A WETLAND MONITORING QAPP, CREATE A WETLAND MONITORING PLAN, AND TRANSFER RESULTS TO OTHER STAKEHOLDERS.  THIS AGREEMENT PROVIDES FULL FEDERAL FUNDING IN THE AMOUNT OF $29,193.

column5: SOUTH COAST AIR QUALITY MANAGEMENT DISTRICT
column8: THE PROJECT WILL DEMONSTRATE BATTERY ELECTRIC NON-ROAD EXCAVATOR AND WHEEL LOADER IN THE SOUTH COAST AIR BASIN (SCAB).  THIS ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT PROVIDES FULL FEDERAL FUNDING IN THE AMOUNT $2,100,000.  PRE-AWARD HAS BEEN APPROVED BACK TO SEPTEMBER 1, 2019.

column5: SOUTH COAST AIR QUALITY MANAGEMENT DISTRICT
column8: DEPLOYMENT OF NEW MEDIUM AND HEAVY-DUTY BATTERY-ELECTRIC DELIVERY TRUCKS IN THE SOUTH COAST AIR BASIN (SCAB).  THIS ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT PROVIDES FULL FEDERAL FUNDING IN THE AMOUNT $4,177,083.  PRE-AWARD COSTS ARE APPROVED BACK TO APRIL 1, 2020.

THIS PROJECT IS FOR THE FURTHER DEVELOPMENT OF THE TRIBES WATER POLLUTION CONTROL PROGRAM BY CONDUCTING WATER QUALITY MONITORING OF TRIBAL WATERS IN ORDER TO ASSESS & MANAGE WATER QUALITY ON THE RESERVATION.  THIS ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT PROVIDES FULL FEDERAL FUNDING IN THE AMOUNT OF $53,315.

THIS AGREEMENT WILL PROVIDE ASSISTANCE TO THE CALIFORNIA AIR RESOURCES BOARD (CARB) IN ITS EFFORTS TO REDUCE DIESEL EMISSIONS AND EXPOSURE THROUGHOUT THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA.  CARB WILL REPLACE DIESEL-FUELED SCHOOL BUSES WITH ZERO-EMISSION ELECTRIC SCHOOL AND/OR OTHER TYPES OF ON-ROAD HEAVY-DUTY DIESEL VEHICLES WITH ZERO-EMISSION VEHICLES, WHICH WILL REDUCE EMISSIONS OF DIESEL PARTICULATE MATTER AND OTHER POLLUTANTS SUCH AS NITROGEN OXIDES.  THIS ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT PROVIDES FULL FEDERAL FUNDING IN THE AMOUNT OF $556,361.

THESE FUNDS SUPPORT THE FURTHER DEVELOPMENT AND ADMINISTRATION OF THE WALKER RIVER PAIUTE TRIBE'S NONPOINT SOURCE POLLUTION CONTROL PROGRAM.  MAJOR ACTIVITIES INCLUDE IMPLEMENTING A SOLAR-POWERED WELL FOR LIVESTOCK, WHICH WILL ULTIMATELY REDUCE NONPOINT SOURCE POLLUTANT LOADS TO THE TRIBAL WATERS THEREBY IMPROVING THE WATER QUALITY OF THE RESERVATION'S WATERS.    THIS ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT PROVIDES FULL FEDERAL FUNDING IN THE AMOUNT OF $130,000.

WOOD STOVE CHANGE-OUT PROGRAM WITH NORTHERN SIERRA AIR QUALITY MANAGEMENT DISTRICT IN THE PORTOLA PM2.5 NONATTAINMENT AREA TO REDUCE AIR EMISSIONS FROM WOOD-BURNING APPLIANCES AND OPEN BURNING, AND ADDRESS QUALITY OF WOOD FUEL IMPROVEMENTS, AND OUTREACH, EDUCATION AND ENFORCEMENT ACTIVITIES.  THIS ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT PROVIDES FULL FEDERAL FUNDING IN THE AMOUNT OF $3,172,238.  PRE-AWARD COSTS ARE APPROVED BACK TO MAY 1, 2020.

THIS AGREEMENT WILL PROVIDE FUNDING FOR THE CITY OF EUREKA TO INVENTORY, CHARACTERIZE, ASSESS, AND CONDUCT CLEANUP PLANNING AND COMMUNITY INVOLVEMENT RELATED ACTIVITIES FOR BROWNFIELD SITES IN A FORMER RAILYARD AREA KNOWN AS THE BALLOON TRACK AND THE SURROUNDING WATERFRONT AREA ADJACENT TO HUMBOLDT BAY. THE CITY WILL ASSESS AND PLAN FOR THE REDEVELOPMENT IN STRATEGIC AREAS THROUGHOUT THE TARGET AREA.  THIS AGREEMENT PROVIDES FULL FEDERAL FUNDING IN THE AMOUNT OF $300,000.

THIS AGREEMENT WILL PROVIDE FUNDING FOR NORTHERN ARIZONA COUNCIL OF GOVERNMENTS TO INVENTORY, CHARACTERIZE, ASSESS, AND CONDUCT CLEANUP PLANNING AND COMMUNITY INVOLVEMENT RELATED ACTIVITIES FOR BROWNFIELD SITES IN THE ROUTE 66 CORRIDOR IN ARIZONA. COALITION MEMBERS ENVISION THE FOLLOWING REDEVELOPMENT ACTIVITIES: 1) PROMOTION OF HISTORIC AND CULTURAL TOURISM OPPORTUNITIES, ESPECIALLY WITH REGARD TO ROUTE 66, RAILROADS, AND UNIQUE NATURAL AND SCENIC RESOURCES; 2) ECONOMIC DIVERSIFICATION TO BROADEN EMPLOYMENT OPPORTUNITIES IN EXISTING AND EMERGING INDUSTRY SECTORS, INCLUDING FOREST RESTORATION AND BIOMASS ENERGY PRODUCTION; AND, 3) REHABILITATION AND/OR CONSTRUCTION OF LOW-TO-MODERATE INCOME AND WORKFORCE HOUSING.  THIS AGREEMENT PROVIDES FULL FEDERAL FUNDING IN THE AMOUNT OF $597,686.

THIS AGREEMENT WILL PROVIDE FUNDING FOR THE WESTERN NEVADA DEVELOPMENT DISTRICT (WNDD) TO INVENTORY, CHARACTERIZE, ASSESS, AND CONDUCT CLEANUP PLANNING AND COMMUNITY INVOLVEMENT RELATED ACTIVITIES FOR BROWNFIELD SITES IN THE CITY OF LOVELOCK AND PORTIONS OF PERSHING COUNTY, NEVADA. SPECIFICALLY, WNDD WILL FOCUS ASSESSMENT WORK ON FOUR TARGET AREAS, INCLUDING COMMUNITY GATEWAYS, INDUSTRIAL PARK, DOWNTOWN CORRIDOR AND MINE SCARRED AREAS. WNDD'S GOAL IS TO SUPPORT ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT AND COMMUNITY REVITALIZATION BY FREEING UP KEY LANDS THAT ARE ALREADY ZONED FOR DEVELOPMENT AND REDUCE SPRAWL BY INFILLING VACANT LOTS WITHIN THE CITY'S LIMITS.  THIS AGREEMENT PROVIDES FULL FEDERAL FUNDING IN THE AMOUNT OF $600,000.  PREAWARD COSTS ARE APPROVED BACK TO 7/1/2019.

THIS AGREEMENT WILL PROVIDE FUNDING FOR THE CITY OF FRESNO, CA TO INVENTORY, CHARACTERIZE, ASSESS, AND CONDUCT CLEANUP PLANNING AND COMMUNITY INVOLVEMENT RELATED ACTIVITIES FOR BROWNFIELD SITES IN THE SOUTH ELM CORRIDOR, DOWNTOWN AND CHINATOWN TARGET AREAS. THE CITY OF FRESNO FORMED A BROWNFIELDS COALITION WITH FRESNO COUNTY, SAINTS REST COMMUNITY ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION AND THE CHINATOWN FRESNO FOUNDATION TO ASSESS AND PLAN FOR THE REDEVELOPMENT IN STRATEGIC AREAS THROUGHOUT THE TARGET AREA. BROWNFIELDS ARE REAL PROPERTY, THE EXPANSION, DEVELOPMENT OR REUSE OF WHICH MAY BE COMPLICATED BY THE PRESENCE OR POTENTIAL PRESENCE OF A HAZARDOUS SUBSTANCE, POLLUTANT, OR CONTAMINANT.  THIS ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT PROVIDES FULL FEDERAL FUNDING IN THE AMOUNT OF $600,000.  SEE TERMS AND CONDITIONS.

THESE FUNDS SUPPORT THE FURTHER DEVELOPMENT AND ADMINISTRATION OF THE MOORETOWN RANCHERIA'S NONPOINT SOURCE POLLUTION CONTROL PROGRAM. MAJOR ACTIVITIES INCLUDE DEVELOPMENT AND IMPLEMENTATION OF BEST MANAGEMENT PRACTICES WHICH WILL ULTIMATELY REDUCE NONPOINT SOURCE POLLUTANT LOADS TO THE TRIBAL WATERS SPECIFICALLY IMPROVING WATER QUALITY OF THE RESERVATION'S WATERS. THIS ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT PROVIDES FEDERAL FUNDING IN THE AMOUNT OF $30,000 FOR THE PERIOD OF 10/1/19-9/30/20.

THIS AGREEMENT WILL PROVIDE FUNDING FOR THE CITY OF PITTSBURG, CALIFORNIA TO CONDUCT A RANGE OF ACTIVITIES ASSOCIATED WITH THE PLANNING, ASSESSMENT, AND CLEANUP OF BROWNFIELD SITES IN THE CITY OF PITTSBURG'S WATERFRONT, DOWNTOWN AND MANUFACTURING CORE (OPPORTUNITY ZONE). THE CITY WILL CONDUCT: CLEANUP PLANS, WORKSHOPS, AND REVITALIZATION PLANS AND WORKSHOPS. BROWNFIELDS ARE REAL PROPERTY, THE EXPANSION, DEVELOPMENT OR REUSE OF WHICH MAY BE COMPLICATED BY THE PRESENCE OR POTENTIAL PRESENCE OF A HAZARDOUS SUBSTANCE, POLLUTANT, OR CONTAMINANT.  THIS ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT PROVIDES FEDERAL FUNDING IN THE AMOUNT OF $800,000.  SEE TERMS AND CONDITIONS.

THIS PROJECT WILL REDUCE EMISSIONS OF DIESEL PARTICULATE MATTER AND OTHER POLLUTANTS SUCH AS NITROGEN OXIDES.  THE STATE OF HAWAII WILL REDUCE DIESEL EMISSIONS BY REPLACING ONE HEAVY-DUTY DIESEL TRUCK AND ONE DIESEL ROAD ZIPPER.   ADDITIONALLY, THEY WILL REPLACE TWO DIESEL BUSES WITH TWO BATTERY-ELECTRIC BUSES. THIS ASSISTANCE AMENDMENT PROVIDES FULL FEDERAL FUNDING IN THE AMOUNT OF $474,741.

DESCRIPTION:THIS COOPERATIVE AGREEMENT CONTINUES THE RECIPIENT'S IMPLEMENTATION OF ITS PESTICIDE REGULATORY AND ENFORCEMENT PROGRAMS, INCLUDING, BUT NOT LIMITED TO, ACTIVITIES SUCH AS: COMPLIANCE MONITORING, ENFORCEMENT, CERTIFICATION AND TRAINING FOR PESTICIDE APPLICATORS, AND OUTREACH AND EDUCATION. THE PURPOSE OF THIS AGREEMENT IS TO SUPPORT THE RECIPIENT'S PESTICIDE PROGRAM PROMOTING ENHANCED PROTECTION OF HUMAN HEALTH AND THE ENVIRONMENT THROUGH REDUCED PESTICIDE EXPOSURE TO THE PUBLIC, AGRICULTURAL WORKERS AND HANDLERS, PROTECTION OF WATER RESOURCES FROM PESTICIDES, AND PROTECTION OF ENDANGERED SPECIES.

THIS ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT PROVIDES FULL FEDERAL FUNDING IN THE AMOUNT OF $45,500. SEE TERMS AND CONDITIONS.ACTIVITIES:THIS AGREEMENT FUNDS PESTICIDE REGULATORY AND ENFORCEMENT ACTIVITIES INCLUDING: TRAINING AND CERTIFYING PESTICIDE APPLICATORS, CONDUCTING WORKER PROTECTION ACTIVITIES TO PROTECT FARMWORKERS AND COMMUNITIES, PROTECTING WATER QUALITY BY IDENTIFYING PESTICIDES OF INTEREST, IDENTIFYING LOCATIONS THAT NEED PROTECTION DUE TO THE LOCATION OF ENDANGERED SPECIES AND THEIR HABITAT, PROTECTING PUBLIC HEALTH FROM UNREGISTERED PESTICIDE PRODUCTS AND MISUSE, MONITORING FOR COMPLIANCE IN THE FIELD AND ENFORCING EPA PESTICIDE LABELS WHEN NEEDED, AND CONDUCTING OUTREACH AND EDUCATION TO THE REGULATED COMMUNITY AND THE PUBLIC.SUBRECIPIENT:NO SUBAWARDS ARE INCLUDED IN THIS ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT.OUTCOMES:ANTICIPATED DELIVERABLES AND EXPECTED OUTCOMES OF THIS COOPERATIVE AGREEMENT INCLUDE BETTER PROTECTION OF PUBLIC HEALTH AND THE ENVIRONMENT THROUGH 1) INCREASED OUTREACH AND EDUCATION ON PROPER PESTICIDE USE TO THE REGULATED COMMUNITY AND THE PUBLIC TO REDUCE RISK, 2) PROPER TRAINING AND CERTIFICATION OF PESTICIDE APPLICATORS, 3) MONITORING FOR COMPLIANCE BY PERFORMING INSPECTIONS, 4) RESPONDING TO TIPS AND COMPLAINTS, AND 5) TAKING ENFORCEMENT ACTION WHEN NEEDED. THE PROGRAM BENEFICIARIES ARE THE REGULATED COMMUNITY AND THE GENERAL PUBLIC.
